title: Programming - Programming in C - Parameter Passing

questions.question:
Various parameter passing mechanisms have been in used in different programming languages. Which of the following statements is true?



Programming in C: TIFR CSE 2011 | Part B | Question: 32
options: Call by value result is used in language Ada.
Call by value result is the same as call by name.
Call by value is the most robust.
Call by reference is the same as call by name.
Call by name is the most efficient.

questions.question:
Consider the following program fragment:

var a,b : integer;
procedure G(c,d: integer);
begin
    c:=c-d;
    d:=c+d;
    c:=d-c
end;
a:=2;
b:=3;
G(a,b);

If both parameters to \( G \) are passed by reference, what are the values of \( a \) and \( b \) at the end of the above program fragment ?



Programming in C: TIFR CSE 2019 | Part B | Question: 8
